key_insight: ClinRAG-GRAPH combines clinical variables, MRI, and pathology with graph learning and retrieval-augmented evidence to improve pre-treatment breast pCR prediction.
clinical_relevance: It may help stratify breast cancer patients before neoadjuvant chemotherapy by identifying those more likely to achieve pathological complete response.
method: Builds an intra-patient clinical-prior graph from DCE-MRI, structured clinical data, and biopsy biomarkers
Uses prior-guided relation-aware graph convolution for multimodal representation learning
Adds dual-branch domain-adversarial learning plus LLM-driven subgraph retrieval for robustness and interpretability
evidence_signal: In multicenter validation, the model reported AUCs of 0.815 on the internal test set and 0.774/0.712 on two external test sets.
watch_item: External performance is lower than internal performance, so real-world deployment may still depend on site-specific imaging protocols and careful prospective validation.
paper_title: ClinRAG-GRAPH: Clinical-prior Retrieval-Augmented Graph Model with Domain Adversarial Learning for Breast pCR Prediction
